_comment: Single source of truth for CI-plane secret rotation (scripts/rotate_secret.py) AND the App-credential distribution (scripts/distribute_app_secrets.py). Each entry is one rotatable secret and lists every place its value lives: GitHub Actions ENVIRONMENT secrets (github_env_secrets: repo + environment + secret_name), GitHub Actions REPOSITORY secrets (github_repo_secrets: repo + secret_name — the store CI reads when a job declares no 'environment:', e.g. the reusable ci-python-*.yml SDK install; a value written only to environments is invisible to those CI jobs), and/or non-Terraform Vercel project env vars (project_id + env_key). 'retired_github_env_secrets' (repo + environment + secret_name) lists env-level copies a secret has MIGRATED AWAY from (now repo-level): the engine DELETEs each on rotation because a leftover env secret shadows the repo value (env wins over repo). Deletes are idempotent (a 404 is success), and for generate secrets the old token is revoked only after every shadow is gone. SCOPE: CI-plane credentials ONLY (build/deploy plumbing). App secrets in Terraform's SECRETS_INVENTORY (DB URLs, RSA keypair, KDF_SERVICE_KEY, STRIPE_*, OIDC client secrets, CRON_SECRET, etc.) are Terraform-owned — do NOT add them here; a direct write would drift. If one is ever added, set "terraform_managed": true and the engine refuses to write it and points to the terraform SECRETS_ROTATION runbook. 'kind' is the intended primary mode (paste|generate|manual|distribute); 'generator' picks the generate strategy (vercel_token|random_urlsafe); 'per_env' makes generate mint a distinct value per environment; 'shared': true on a vercel_token entry mints ONE token (entry-level 'vercel_token_name') and writes the same value to every target (app/env filters ignored). 'distribute_source_env' opts an entry into distribute_app_secrets.py (ops:distribute-app-secrets): the value is read from that env var (wired by the ops workflow from THIS repo's same-named secret) and fanned out to the entry's github_repo_secrets — the target list doubles as the org-move cleanup checklist for the per-repo copies; 'value_format' (numeric|pem) is its pre-write shape check. Vercel entries whose project_id starts with 'TODO' are skipped with a warning.

secrets.name: GH_PACKAGES_PAT
secrets.description: Fine-grained PAT (contents:read over the owner's repos — at minimum the private package repos kriegerdataforge-sdk + kriegerdataforge-reports-sdk) used at build time to pip-install the private packages (kdf_sdk, kdf_reports). GitHub cannot mint PATs via API, so this is paste-only: owner creates the PAT, stages it in the SECRET_VALUE_NEW repo secret, then runs paste. There is ONE global PAT, so it lives ONLY as a REPOSITORY-level Actions secret (github_repo_secrets) — which every job can read, including CI jobs that declare no 'environment:' (the reusable ci-python-*.yml SDK install) AND CD jobs (a job with an environment reads repo + env secrets together). It is deliberately NOT stored per-environment: a same-named env secret would SHADOW the repo value (env wins). The former per-environment copies were already deleted in the one-time repo-level migration, so this token no longer carries retired_github_env_secrets (nothing left to reap). All five package-consuming repos (kriegerdataforge, fitness-app-backend, tiffanys-space-backend, the scaffold kriegerdataforge-template-fastapi, and kriegerdataforge-reports-sdk — whose own CI installs kdf_sdk as a peer dep) install via this repo-level secret. Since epic W2.5 the ci-python-* lanes prefer a short-lived App installation token when the caller opted in (USE_GITHUB_APP) — this PAT remains the fallback + the local-dev/Vercel-build credential. (Vercel project env vars below are a separate build-time store and are left as-is.)

secrets.name: GH_NPM_TOKEN
secrets.description: CLASSIC PAT with ONLY the read:packages scope, used to install the private GH-Packages npm packages (@needless2say/report-form — @kriegerdataforge/* after the org move). It exists because GitHub Packages accepts ONLY classic PATs or the Actions GITHUB_TOKEN: fine-grained PATs are rejected, so GH_PACKAGES_PAT (fine-grained) can never serve npm, and GitHub Apps have no Packages permission at all (reports-ecosystem epic W3 spike, docs-verified 2026-07). One global value, stored as a REPOSITORY-level Actions secret on the frontend consumer repos (the ci-nextjs-*/cd-nextjs-vercel lanes export it for `npm ci`; a consumer's committed .npmrc interpolates ${GH_NPM_TOKEN}) and as a Vercel project env var on the frontend projects (build-time). Vercel entries below carry TODO project ids until filled (the engine skips TODOs with a warning); Terraform backfill of the Vercel side is a post-epic review item. Where possible prefer the ZERO-secret alternative for consumer CI: a per-repo Actions-access grant on the package (see the npm template's PRIVATE_GH_PACKAGES guide). GitHub cannot mint PATs via API, so this is paste-only. When the owner first mints it, add a check block ({ "expiry": "YYYY-MM-DD", "warn_days_before_expiry": 14 }) so the weekly monitor tracks it. Recipe: docs/guides/SECRET_ROTATION.md §8.2a.

secrets.name: VERCEL_DEPLOYMENT_TOKEN
secrets.description: ONE shared, account-scoped Vercel token used EVERYWHERE: every app repo's CI/CD deploys with it AND the terraform repo uses it to MANAGE Vercel (create projects, set env vars) via the Terraform provider. Consolidated 2026-06-30 from the former split (VERCEL_TOKEN deploy + VERCEL_API_TOKEN terraform) — both were the same Full-Account-privilege token, so two separate tokens was pure redundancy. Because there is ONE value everywhere it is stored ONLY as a REPOSITORY-level secret per repo (github_repo_secrets), NOT per-environment: a same-named env secret would SHADOW the repo value (env wins over repo), so the former prod/dev env copies are listed under retired_github_env_secrets and DELETED on rotation. CD jobs keep 'environment:' for their OTHER per-env secrets (VERCEL_PROJECT_ID, DB URLs, keys) and read this token from repo-level in the same job. Auto-minted via the Vercel API as a single token, written to every repo target; the previous token is revoked only if ALL writes succeed AND every retired env shadow was removed (otherwise both stay valid so no repo holds a revoked token). App/env filters are ignored — it is one value everywhere. In the terraform repo this secret feeds TF_VAR_vercel_api_token (the provider's variable name is unchanged). Its check.expiry is AUTO-MAINTAINED: after each mint the rotation workflow runs `--mode record-expiry` to stamp today+45d and opens a PR; the weekly monitor then doubles as a watchdog if the cron ever stops.

secrets.name: CICD_PAT
secrets.description: Fine-grained PAT carrying the full provisioning/fallback scope: Repository Administration + Contents + Environments + Secrets + Variables + Actions + Issues + Pull requests (all read/write) over ALL repos (incl. future), plus Metadata read. The rotation + kit workflows mint ephemeral GitHub App tokens (docs/design/github-app-migration.md) and use CICD_PAT only as the `|| secrets.CICD_PAT` fallback; its sole ACTIVE consumer is issue-create-repo.yml (creating a personal-account repo needs a user PAT — an App token can't, until the org move). GitHub has no PAT-creation API, so it is rotated BY HAND — monitored only (no engine targets). Retirement is tracked in the central roadmap (KDF docs/engineering/GITHUB_FUTURE_ENHANCEMENTS.md, Enhancement 1). Recipe: docs/guides/SECRET_ROTATION.md §8.3. Last rotated 2026-06-30 with a 30-day token.

secrets.name: VERCEL_MASTER_TOKEN
secrets.description: Full-Account Vercel token the engine uses to mint/delete the shared deploy/management token (VERCEL_DEPLOYMENT_TOKEN). Stays MANUAL (paste-only): minting requires Full Account scope, and this is the bootstrap credential the rotation itself depends on — it can't rotate itself with itself. Rotate it by hand before expiry (recipe §8.4) and update check.expiry below. Monitored only (no engine targets). Last rotated 2026-06-30 with a 30-day Vercel token.

secrets.name: KDF_APP_ID
secrets.description: The KDF GitHub App's numeric id — not sensitive by itself, but stored as a secret alongside KDF_APP_PRIVATE_KEY so the pair travels together. Consumer repos need their OWN copy (personal-account repos can't share secrets until the org move): the copies feed the run-e2e composite action's App-token mint and the ci-python-* (W3: ci-nextjs-*) private-package install tokens. Fanned out by scripts/distribute_app_secrets.py via the ops:distribute-app-secrets issue flow; the source of truth is THIS repo's same-named secret. The target list below is the authoritative inventory of every repo holding a copy — after the org move it is the cleanup checklist (delete the copies, use org secrets). The App id itself never rotates (only the private key does), so there is no check block.

secrets.name: KDF_APP_PRIVATE_KEY
secrets.description: Private key (.pem) for the KDF GitHub App, stored as a kriegerdataforge-cicd repo secret (alongside KDF_APP_ID). The rotation + kit workflows mint short-lived installation tokens from it (actions/create-github-app-token) instead of using long-lived PATs. It has no expiry, but rotate it periodically; GitHub Apps support multiple private keys for zero-downtime rotation. Consumer repos hold copies (github_repo_secrets — same 12-repo list as KDF_APP_ID; the pair always travels together) fanned out by ops:distribute-app-secrets; AFTER rotating this repo's secret, re-run that flow or the consumer copies keep minting from the old key and break the moment it is deleted. Recipe: docs/guides/SECRET_ROTATION.md §8.3a.
